entry.title: Re: Attachments with email not going
entry.content:
Hi ramco1917,

Are you using SAP Business One client or Service Layer for the same?

There is a known issue documented in SAP Note 3421588 if you are using Service Layer.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: In scwm/vasexec, I am not able to see the vas orders
entry.content: Thanks , Your input was lot helpful.. Really appreciated.

entry.title: Re: 14 AI Agents topics you didn't know you needed
entry.content: @Virinchy I agree! SAP will allow us to extend Joule, but limited to API extensibility. We are still not allowed to adapt Joule to customer needs, nor (this is an ambitious game) be able to adapt Joule as the time progresses. not to blame SAP, that is a complex technology requirement, which requires to open Joule at its heart, or wait until the technology is mature enough you can preserve the core while the external components of Joule are mature enough to create enterprise scale applications. Until then, Joule has limited knowledge of the SAP data (might know what comes through the prompt) nor the ability to adapt to the learnings of the interactions with a certain company. But, honestly, who has that today? which enterprise process vendor offers that today?

entry.title: How to check not allocate amount after material ledger costing run
entry.content:
Hello Community!

How to check not allocate amount after material ledger costing run

Material Ledger & Actual Costing activated company

The material ledger settlement is completed without errors.

In the Material Value Flow Monitor app, an amount is recorded under Not Allocated for Cost Center and Activity Type 1 (Machine Hour).

 

 

Display Cost Center report app.

After manufacturing overhead allocation, the remaining balance is -57 KRW. 

How should I check the Not Allocated Amount in Cost Center/ActivityType?

Huny

entry.title: Re: Replacement of Invalid JSON Element that fails JSON t...
entry.content:
try with the following:

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper
import groovy.xml.MarkupBuilder
import groovy.xml.XmlUtil

def processData(Message message) {
    // Read JSON payload from message body
    def jsonString = message.getBody(String)

    // Parse JSON payload
    def json = new JsonSlurper().parseText(jsonString)


    if (json.Attributes?.containsKey("Ignore Volume?")) {
        json.Attributes["ignoreVolume"] = json.Attributes.remove("Ignore Volume?")
    }

    // Create XML structure
    def writer = new StringWriter()
    def xml = new MarkupBuilder(writer)

    xml.Root {  // Root element
        json.each { key, value ->
            if (value instanceof Map) {
                "$key" {
                    value.each { subKey, subValue ->
                        "$subKey"(subValue)
                    }
                }
            } else {
                "$key"(value)
            }
        }
    }

    // Pretty-print XML
    def xmlString = writer.toString()

    message.setBody(xmlString)

    return message
}

entry.title: Re: RAP On-Stack Developer Extensibility
entry.content:
@SampathReddy  yes "allowNewCompositions: true" must be enabled for standard RAP BO CDS Views then only you can enable node extensions. 

In higher version of S/4HANA may be above 2023 version you can see this annotations enabled for many RAP BO CDS Views.

entry.title: Is there a way to handle grandfathered cases in the annual merit increase in successfactors
entry.content:
Hi

Is there a way to handle grandfathered cases in the annual merit increase cycle in successfactors. In Compensation by standard we map the position job grade and system shows the pay ranges and bench marking (Range penetration and Compa-ratio). 

But in grandfathered cases the pay ranges and bench marking calculation should be based on the grandfathered grade which can be higher than the current grade.

Any thoughts on this, please let me know.

Regards

Vivek

Thanks,

Rotem

entry.title: How to remove the delete option from Personal Data Collection

Hi, 

I want to prevent onboardees from deleting address information during the personal data collection step in Onboarding? Any suggestions for the best way to achieve this?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Create a Nested IF Else calculated key figure (KF) th...
entry.content:
Hi @SoniaMarwah,

The first thing I would like to add is that you are using IF("PERIODID3" < "$$PERIODID3CU$$" -1,... and not IF("PERIODID3" = "$$PERIODID3CU$$" -1,... The former is slightly different from your wording ''If current period is current period-1, then... otherwise...'.

With regards to your main query, you can build in another condition to check for "snapshot 1@MTHPRODLOCCUST" being equal to 0. Please note that NULL and 0 are not the same. It sounds like, in your case, you want to have both conditions used in your calculation.

I hope this helped.

Kind regards,

Vincent Verheyen

entry.title: Re: SAP project system
entry.content: Date parameters are available in dynamic selection section of CN48N, you can select this by clicking dynamic selection button right to execution button in the toolbar. By the way, are you looking for any specific date field in the selection screen?

entry.title: Re: BW cockpit is not working
entry.content:
Hi,

did you run the task list SAP_BW4_AFTER_UPGRADE? It includes a task to activate the cockpit.
An important step, which is too often overlooked unfortunately!

Also helpful to know these two transactions:
BW4WEB to lauch the cockpit
BW4CHECK to test if all services are active

KR Frank

Error importing object ID: RESOURCE:t.G:36B84E01CD0F49EF7C393F3124EF436C

Correlation ID: 31357238-4915-4493-9158-042277155512

How do I solve that?

thanks!

Best regards,

tedi

entry.title: Re: How to freeze a key figure for period of time?
entry.content:
Hi @alicia25 ,

When you say 'freeze', is the context a Stored, editable Key Figure, and do you mean that you want certain / all users to not be able to edit the Key Figures during certain months?

In that case, the most standard way to achieve this is by using the 'Editability Horizon for Key Figures'.

If you want to read up on the functionality, please refer to SAP Help > IBP > User Authorizations and Permissions > Data Filters > Editability Horizons for Key Figures > Manage Editability Horizons for Key Figures.

In order to accelerate your prorject, I will give you some quick implementation guidance here:
- Step 1: 'Create' in the IBP web-UI app 'Editability Horizon for Key Figures', for your Planning Area, and assign the Key Figure(s) you want to restrict there. For Horizon, choose your Time Period, for example 'Month', apply a Fixed Rule from Month 3 To Unlimited. Also, assign Users and/or Business Roles there. This way, those users will only be able to edit the months mentioned in your Fixed Rule.
- Step 2: In order for the above to be effective, for example for the relevant Business Roles, you will need to also go to the IBP web-UI app 'Maintain Business Roles',  go into 'Edit' > 'Maintain Restrictions', in 'GENERAL' > 'Editability Horizon', put a 'Write' restriction for the Editability Horizon you created in Step 1.

You could turn the 'Editability Horizon' on or off when needed.

If you have more information regarding the context of your freeze, please feel free to inform me, and then I could update my answer according to your specific context.

I hope this helped.

Kind regards,

Vincent Verheyen

entry.title: Fiori no app display
entry.content:
Hi Expert:

I try to add APP to my user. but nothing display.

Pls advise.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: IBP transporting changes
entry.content:
Hi @aparnau .

1. There is no need to create an OSS note / SAP Case every time you want to export a change from an IBP Dev/Test system into your IBP Production system. Setting up the transport route is a one time activity. There is no need to reach out to SAP subsequently.

2. Whether or not they are Planning Area related changes, you can move them together in 1 Software Collection, or split them into different/multiple Software Collections according to your preference. This is done in the IBP web-UI app 'Export Software Collection'. You can simply 'Add Items' of any 'Type' to a Software Collection; and in case you only want to export this item, or a few items only, then simply select the item(s) subsequently and 'Add to Hotfix'. Subsequently, one can export only the Hotfix. Then open the target (in your case IBP Production) tenant/system, and via the web-UI app 'Import Collection' you can complete the Import. As I am sure you are aware, in case these are Planning Area-related changes, what is left to do is to activate the Planning Area in the Production tenant (outside business hours / during a window agreed upon with your customer), to effectuate the changes.

I hope this helped.

Kind regards,
Vincent Verheyen

entry.title: Using the List view to your advantage
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One community,

In this new series I provide ideas how to make the most out of the SAP Business One Web client solution.

I invite you to join the ride 😊

Today I want to talk about the list view, which is an amazingly tool for managing your data, creating you own customized reports and much more.

Did you know that you could perform in the list view batch actions, e.g. Close several Sales Orders with a click or save a filtered list of transactions as a new view ?

Let’s look at this video to learn more.

You can find additional information in the SAP Business One user guide and the Learning journey.

Regards

Ari

entry.title: Re: February's Developer Challenge in SAP Community - Integration 🧩

entry.title: Rate Limiting on SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 APIs - Must to know
entry.content:
When performing an initial data load in SAP Sales Cloud and Service Cloud Version 2 (CNS), transferring a large volume of data can sometimes lead to encountering API rate limits. This can result in messages failing with the Error Code: 429 in SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) or SAP Integration Suite. Understanding and managing these rate limits is crucial for a smooth data migration process.

What is Rate Limiting? 🚀

Rate limiting is a technique that controls how many requests a user or a client can make to an API in a given period. For example, you might limit a Technical user to 10000 requests per hour, or 1000 requests per minute. The purpose of rate limiting is to prevent abuse, overload, or misuse of your API.

Why Rate Limiting is in place for SAP Sales & Service Cloud API? 👀

Rate limiting is not only a security measure, but also a way to improve the performance, reliability, and user experience of CNS API. Benefits on rate limiting, can be :

  • Reduce the load on your server: By limiting the number of requests, it can prevent your server from being overwhelmed by too much traffic, which can cause slowdowns, errors, or crashes.
  • Save bandwidth and resources: By limiting the number of requests, it can reduce the amount of data and processing power that your API consumes, which can save you money and improve efficiency.
  • Prevent unfair usage: By limiting the number of requests, can ensure that all users have equal access to your API and that no one can monopolize it or take advantage of it.
  • Encourage good practices: By limiting the number of requests, you can motivate users to use your API responsibly and efficiently and follow the best practices and guidelines you provide.

 

Understanding API Rate Limits on SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2

API rate limits are imposed to ensure the stability and performance of the system for all users. When the number of API requests exceeds the allowed limit within a specific time frame, the system responds with a 429 error code, indicating that the rate limit has been exceeded

Send an initial data load from an on-premise system to Sales & Service Cloud V2.

  1. Open the URL of your CPI URL system.
  2. Go to Monitor view
  3. Under the Monitor Message Processing, open All Artifacts tile

You will find the respective Artifact Name (iflow) on the Failed state with the Error Details: Status Code: 429

Note: 429 Error Code means Too Many Request

You have exceeded the rate limits of API Calls for your tenant within 24 hours; This blog aims to elucidate the process you have to follow while performing initial data load from SAP S4HANA or SAP S4HANA Cloud or SAP ERP into Sales & Service Cloud v2 and when it over exceeds the limit of API Calls(services part of Communication configuration / data-connector enabled) outlined in the supplement document, part of an agreement for SAP Cloud Sales and Services Version 2.

As per the SAP Commercial Model, you can only send 150.000 messages, plus an additional 1,500 messages per licensed User, within the 24-hour time frame, from S/4 to Sales and Service Cloud V2.

SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 Supplemental Terms and Conditions outline the following:

"The total number of API and event calls related to the Cloud Service per 24-hour period is limited to 150,000 plus an additional 1,500 per licensed User. This includes all API calls for the Cloud Service except the standard Cloud Service User Interface. Data import and exports can only be done one at a time, and not in batches. Each import or export cannot exceed 50,000 records per block." 

Please Note: For Production Tenants, the number of allowed calls is calculated as described above; whereas for Test Tenants the limit is 30000 per day.

entry.title: An application error occurred. Please try again later, or notify support if the issue keeps occurrin
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community!
So I'm new to this SAP learning hub and have been following the learning journey for SAP THR81/ SuccessFactors EC in my practice instance.

What I've observed is that every time I try to follow any assignment related to imports, I get this error- An application error occurred. Please try again later, or notify support if the issue keeps occurring.

This same error poppped in Provisioning while I was trying to upload Corporate and CSF CDM, now  tried to import a location using import foundation data and the same error is there. I know for the fact that if there's anything wronhg with my values input, it would throw different kinds of errors, which need to be analyzed but this seems to be with the product/ practice instance.

Has anybody dealt with such a thing before? Any suggestions that might help resolve are welcome 🙂

entry.title: CAPM Basics - Part 1
entry.content:
Hello Colleagues,

Below blog will guide you on how to create a basic CAP application and deploy the application to a persistent SQLite DB. 

Sign in to your global account and launch BAS from your instances and subscriptions.

Create a new Dev Space and select a "Full Stack Cloud Application". This will pre-install the necessary extensions/libraries.

Your new Dev Space is now created. Launch it.

 

 

Browse to the "projects" directory of your Dev Space and open the same.

In the Projects directory, create a new CAP project as shown below. Open terminal and use the command "cds init <project_name>". A sample CAP application with the pre-defined folder structure is created.

In the "db" folder, create a file named "data-model.cds" and paste the contents as below. This creates the entity "trainer" in the namespace "training". 

namespace training;

using {cuid} from '@sap/cds/common';

define entity trainer : cuid {
    name        : String;
    location    : String;
    designation : String

}

In the "srv" folder, create a file named "services.cds". This creates a OData V4 service exposes the entity as a view/projection. 

using training as db from '../db/data-model';

@path: 'MyService'
service CatalogService {
    entity trainers  as projection on db.trainer;
}

Use "cds add data" to add data feature to your project. This will be the sample mock data for testing our application in development.

Add some sample data to the csv file.

From the terminal, run "cds watch" and observe the logs. Our mock data is loaded from the csv file and deployed to in-memory database. This is the default configuration in CAP. Run the application by clicking on the highlighted URL.

The default HTML page of your CAP application loads up. Click on the entity "trainers". A "GET" request is fired to fetch the data for the same entity.

Now return to your CAP application, and create a "test.http" file in the root of your project as shown below.

Write a GET request as shown below and execute the same.

Now write a POST request and execute the same. Hit the "GET all records" request again. Observe a new record is created with a guid assigned by CAP. This is because we used a cuid aspect for our entity which assigns a unique primary key to our entity.

Now stop your CAP application (hit CTRL+C in your terminal), start it again (execute cds watch in your terminal) and execute the above "GET all records" once more. What happens? We only get the records from our csv file. The record that we posted in the previous step is lost. This is because we are using a default in-memory database (ephemeral database) as observed from our logs previously.

To "fix" this, we will now shift to a persistent SQLite DB. To make CAP understand that we want to use a persistent DB instead of a in-memory DB, add the following lines in your package.json file.

{ "cds": { "requires": {
   "db": {
      "kind": "sqlite",
      "credentials": { "url": "db.sqlite" } 
   }
}}}

 

Now execute the command "cds deploy --to sqlite" as shown below. A new file db.sqlite is created which is now our local persistent DB.

Execute "cds watch" to start the application again. Observe the logs. The application is now connected to our persistent SQLite DB instead of in-memory database.

Now POST a new record, stop the application, start the application again and execute the "GET all records" request. What do you observe? The record that was posted is persisted even after restarting the application.

So now you have a fully capable CRUD application service with very minimum development. If you have any questions, please feel free to comment below.

entry.title: Understanding SAP Stocks: Logical Stock, MRO, and Physical Stock
entry.content:
Hello All,

We were for a new engagement; while finalizing the data object list, we encountered the different types of stocks under Production Planning (PP) module. 

Let us quickly go through the stock objects and the purpose of them.

In the world of SAP (Systems, Applications, and Products in Data Processing), effective inventory management is crucial for optimizing supply chain operations. SAP offers a robust system to manage different types of stocks, which include

  1. Logical Stock,
  2. MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) Stock, and
  3. Physical Stock

Understanding the differences among them is key to efficient resource utilization and inventory control. In this blog, we’ll explore each type in detail, along with real-time examples to illustrate their application in businesses.

1. Logical Stock

Definition:

Logical Stock in SAP refers to the stock that exists in the system but is not physically available in the warehouse. It is essentially a virtual representation of inventory that helps in planning and reserving stock for specific purposes, like sales orders or production. Logical stock ensures accurate stock visibility and avoids double-booking.

Types of Logical Stock:

  • Blocked Stock: Stock that is not available for use due to quality inspection or other reasons.
  • Reserved Stock: Stock reserved for specific sales orders or production orders.
  • Consignment Stock: Stock owned by a vendor but stored at the company’s premises.

Real-Time Example:

Consider a manufacturing company that produces smartphones. They receive a batch of display screens that need quality inspection before they can be used in production. In SAP, these screens are moved to Blocked Stock, ensuring they are not allocated for any other purpose until cleared for use.

Similarly, if a customer places a large order, the required number of screens can be moved to Reserved Stock to ensure they are not used for any other orders. This helps in fulfilling commitments without stock discrepancies.

2. MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) Stock

Definition:

MRO Stock refers to inventory used for maintenance, repair, and operational activities rather than for production or sales. These are non-product-specific items like spare parts, lubricants, tools, and cleaning supplies essential for keeping manufacturing equipment running smoothly.

Characteristics:

  • Not part of the final product but critical for operational continuity.
  • Generally, low-cost items but vital for maintenance activities.
  • Stored separately from production inventory for easy access by the maintenance team.

Real-Time Example:

A chemical processing plant uses various pumps and machinery that require regular maintenance. The MRO Stock includes spare pump seals, lubricants, safety gear, and cleaning agents. These items are not directly related to production but are crucial for maintaining equipment efficiency.

In SAP, MRO Stock is managed using Material Type "ERSA" (Spare Parts) or "FHMI" (Production Resources/Tools). This ensures they are available whenever needed without affecting production schedules.

3. Physical Stock

Definition:

Physical Stock refers to the actual inventory present in the warehouse. It is the stock that can be physically counted and verified. In SAP, physical stock is recorded in real-time and adjusted during goods receipt, issue, or stocktaking.

Types of Physical Stock:

  • Unrestricted Stock: Available for immediate use in production or sales.
  • Quality Inspection Stock: Stock under quality check before becoming unrestricted.
  • Blocked Stock: Physically present but not available for use due to defects or other reasons.

Real-Time Example:

A retail company dealing in electronics receives a shipment of laptops. Upon arrival, the laptops are entered into SAP as Unrestricted Stock, making them available for sale. If a few units show damaged packaging, they are moved to Blocked Stock for further inspection.

During a periodic stocktaking activity, the physical count of laptops is compared with the SAP records. Any discrepancies are adjusted to reflect accurate inventory levels, ensuring reliable stock visibility and financial reporting.

Key Differences Among Logical, MRO, and Physical Stock

AspectLogical StockMRO StockPhysical Stock
DefinitionVirtual representation of stock for planning purposesInventory used for maintenance and repairActual stock physically present in the warehouse
UsageReservation, quality checks, consignmentMaintenance, repair, and operational supportProduction, sales, or direct usage
VisibilityExists only in SAP system; no physical presencePhysically present but not used in productionPhysically counted and verified
ExampleReserved Stock for customer ordersSpare parts for machinery maintenanceLaptops available for sale in a retail store

 

Conclusion:

Understanding the differences among Logical Stock, MRO Stock, and Physical Stock in SAP is crucial for effective inventory management. Logical Stock aids in planning and reservation without affecting physical inventory. MRO Stock ensures uninterrupted maintenance and operational activities, while Physical Stock represents the tangible inventory available for production or sales.

By leveraging these stock categories effectively, businesses can optimize supply chain operations, reduce stock discrepancies, and enhance overall productivity.

Final Thoughts:

Efficient stock management is the backbone of successful logistics and supply chain operations. Implementing the right SAP strategies for Logical, MRO, and Physical Stock can significantly improve inventory accuracy and operational efficiency.

For businesses looking to enhance their inventory management practices, understanding and utilizing these stock types is a game-changer. Want to know more about SAP inventory solutions? Feel free to reach out or drop your comments below!

Thanks,
Venkatesh Golla

entry.title: Rounding off asset depreciation values and not considering decimals
entry.content:
Hi All

I've observed that the system is rounding off asset depreciation values and not considering decimals. Could you please advise on how to configure S/4HANA Cloud to display decimal places for depreciation values?

Should I need to select Rounding Basis as "No Rounding" in the Define Depreciation Areas"?

 

Thanks 
Naveen

entry.title: Re: Method to Disable the "Access Recent Search" Feature...
entry.content:
Hi Jyoo,

Recent Search feature was added with the 2502 version of SAP Business ByDesign, and it is currently not possible to disable it.

You can also refer to KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/3574152

Thanks and Regards,

Pavithra

Sambit

entry.title: Re: Call an OData Web API Service externally
entry.content:
Hi Remi Kaimal,

Thank you for quick reply.

So, you propose, additionally setup a Destination.

I've set up the Destination:

URL - I put URL of my OData Web API

Client ID and password - my SAP BTP account.

Token Service URL - URL+/oauth/token from XSUAA service key

Token Service user - clientid from XSUAA service key

Token Service password - clientsecret from XSUAA service key

However, check connection issues following:

 

Thank you for the provided python code. Let me share with you my code:

import requests

# Data XSUAA
client_id = "*************************"
client_secret = "**********************************************"
token_url = "https://****************************************/oauth/token"

# User data
username = "nevigon@gmail.com"
password = "**********"

# 1. getting token
token_response = requests.post(
    token_url,
    data={
        "grant_type": "client_credentials",
    },
    auth=(client_id, client_secret)
)

if token_response.status_code == 200:
    access_token = token_response.json().get("access_token")
    print("✅ Token received")
else:
    print("❌ Token error:", token_response.status_code, token_response.text)
    exit()

# 2. OData API call
odata_url = "https://******************API URL*******************"
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}",
    "Accept": "application/json",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

response = requests.get(odata_url, headers=headers)

# Check the response
print("Code:", response.status_code)
print("Server response:", response.text)

if response.status_code == 200:
    try:
        print(response.json())  # Convert to JSON
    except requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError:
        print("❌ Error. No JSON response.")
else:
    print("❌ Error response:", response.status_code, response.text)

I successfully receive the tokens. Let me provide a decoded one:

{
  "alg": "RS256",
  "jku": "https://b1425eebtrial.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/token_keys",
  "kid": "default-jwt-key-c42c936ca8",
  "typ": "JWT",
  "jid": "nWwabhC2sx5tfM+QksEpyq21QuQVeZXO7u+RbCZ/0N4="
}.{
  "jti": "3f8509858de34e269bde70930bc360f0",
  "ext_attr": {
    "enhancer": "XSUAA",
    "subaccountid": "dac0d334-d3ec-4c62-8a82-45f149c54114",
    "zdn": "b1425eebtrial"
  },
  "sub": "sb-xsuaa_iot!t404407",
  "authorities": [
    "uaa.resource",
    "xsuaa_iot!t404407.Viewer",
    "xsuaa_iot!t404407.Admin"
  ],
  "scope": [
    "uaa.resource",
    "xsuaa_iot!t404407.Admin",
    "xsuaa_iot!t404407.Viewer"
  ],
  "client_id": "sb-xsuaa_iot!t404407",
  "cid": "sb-xsuaa_iot!t404407",
  "azp": "sb-xsuaa_iot!t404407",
  "grant_type": "client_credentials",
  "rev_sig": "92b63c51",
  "iat": 1740316573,
  "exp": 1740359773,
  "iss": "https://b1425eebtrial.authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token",
  "zid": "dac0d334-d3ec-4c62-8a82-45f149c54114",
  "aud": [
    "sb-xsuaa_iot!t404407",
    "uaa",
    "xsuaa_iot!t404407"
  ]
}.[Signature]

The problem is the API does not accept the token. I push it to the header. And unfortunately don't receive the JSON response as I received via browser.

I can't understand, where is my mistake here. Probably, SAP BTP Trial has some hidden limitations...

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
This Comment is going to be a container for information about SAP Business AI S/4HANA Embedded AI Agentic AI....

To do SAP Business AI S/4HANA Embedded AI Agentic AI, you need to be a RISE or GROW Customer and to have licensed AI Units, that's the basic pre-requisite, and then of course for the Functional Domains, where applicable you'll need to have the license for that Function Module...

S/4HANA Embedded AI, or Agentic AI is the really interesting one, because this is where the AI Agents are improving the Business Processes and ultimately where AI Agents in different Functions will work together in the End to End Business Process...

This ties in again to the renaissance of the term, SAP Business Suite, start by reading this document... 

The SAP Business Suite Advantage

https://dam.sap.com/mac/app/p/pdf/asset/preview/mEz8n1W?h=&ltr=a

 

 

 

 

What is SAP Business Suite? Business Transformation | SAP

SAP Business Suite | Unmatched AI, Data, and Applications

The above page links to a document...  "Explore what's next for AI Innovation in ERP" which is here https://www.sap.com/uk/documents/2024/11/b4b913ce-e57e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html that document, the "IDC FutureScape: Worldwide Intelligent ERP Predictions"  is another... must read, that's where we are going as is this one, Maximizing the value of ERP with AI and Generative AI and that document then links to this one, "Business AI: Relevant Reliable Responsible" https://www.sap.com/registration/protected/default-overlay.html?gated_asset_path=%2F%2Fwww.sap.com%2Fdocuments%2F2024%2F06%2F48d2fbc6-c67e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.urc.html&pdf-asset=48d2fbc6-c67e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b&page=1 

This page is a go to site for SAP Business AI and explains all of the different types of AI and all of the Use Cases https://www.sap.com/uk/products/artificial-intelligence.html

BTW SAP Global AI Ethics Policy SAP Global AI Ethics Policy

entry.title: Assembly Subcontracting Process in Production Planning - SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Subcontracting—a process where certain production activities or components are outsourced to external vendors while maintaining seamless integration with in-house operations. For companies leveraging SAP S/4 HANA, the subcontracting process within Production Planning (PP) offers a robust framework to manage these external operations effectively.

In this blog, we’ll explore the intricacies of the Assembly subcontracting process in SAP S/4 HANA Production Planning. We’ll cover key concepts, configuration steps, and best practices to help you implement and manage subcontracting efficiently. Whether you’re new to SAP S/4 HANA or looking to refine your existing processes, this guide will provide valuable insights to enhance your production planning capabilities.

Assembly Subcontracting: The assembly subcontracting process begins with the creation of an automatic subcontracting “Purchase Request” from MRP which then processed to purchase order, which specifies the materials and components to be sent to the vendor. Once the vendor completes the assembly, the finished goods are received back into the system, and the consumed components are automatically posted as consumed. This end-to-end process is tightly integrated with inventory management, procurement, and production planning, ensuring real-time visibility and accurate tracking of materials and costs.

Step 1: Set up Special Procurement Key

Path: SPRO > Production -> Materials Requirements Planning -> Master data -> Define special procurement type.

Step 2: Maintain Special Procurement Key in Header Material

Step 3: Create BOM for Header Material

Step 4: Requirement for Planning in MD61

Step 5: MRP Run for Header Material in MD02

Step 6: Convert the Auto Generated PRs into POs in MD04

Available stock as on 23.02.2025 is 12 PC.

Planned Requirement

02/25 – 10 PC (No PR created sufficient stock available)

03/25 – 20 PC (2 PC available, PR created for Remaining 8 PC)

04/25 – 30 PC (0 PC Available, PR created for 30 PC)

Display BOM

As mentioned in BOM, 1 Final product requires 25 PC of Raw Material

PR & PO created for 8 PC & 30 PC

8 PC Final product requires 200 PC of Raw Material

30 PC Final product requires 750 PC of Raw Material

Step 7: Issue Components to PO Material in MIGO

541 Movement Type – Transfer the stock from Unrestricted to Subcontracting Stock

Step 8: Goods Receipt for Header Material

Enter the Cost Center for components

Enter the Location for Final Product in which stock will get updated.

Components stock gets consumed once after posting the MIGO document

Final Product Stock gets updated in the mentioned storage location.

entry.title: Re: Why sap:external-function is not allowed in the Cloud?
entry.content: well - the task behind this question - is to build an effective JSON serializer, parser ( may be even with XML too ). Of course - I can build JSON, load it to sXML model and transform - but that's something I want actually to avoid to do. performance wise transformations work much faster. So this question was not about how to do this - it was mainly a call - that again after hundreds of different reasons we cannot run in a cloud ABAP something which was working for decades.. Personally I do not see a difference between sap:call-external or sap:external-function and changing the code made it working exactly same way. But still - this is the manual action which has to be done to keep transformation working. Additionally as you can see in the shared link - i took it from the official documentation. https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/abenabap_xslt_abap_calls.html. So it means that I want to use something which is documented - but ADT doesn't even allow to compile..

entry.title: Manage Service Entry Sheets - Lean Services ( App ID:Issue With Drop-Down Field In Extended CDS View
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I got the requirement to add a new custom field i.e., Penalties in the Manage Service Entry Sheets - Lean Services (App ID:F2027). To achieve this functionality, added the Penalty field to the SES Header table MMPUR_SES_HEADER-ZZ_PENALTIES, using the MMPUR_SES_HEADER_INCL_EEW_PS existing persistence structure. Later, to make the field visible in the UI layer, extended the CDS views in the following manner.

  1. Added penalties field in the extended SES CDS view E_SERVICEENTRYSHEET 

     

  2. In The Interface View I_ServiceEntrySheetTP consumed the penalties field via E_SERVICEENTRYSHEET

     

     

  3. In the Consumption CDS view C_ServiceEntrySheetWD consumed the penalties field via I_ServiceEntrySheetTP
     

     

Now I am able to view the penalties field in the Manage Service Entry Sheets - Lean Services application as user entry during the CREATE operation.

To make the penalties field a drop-down field, I have created a new CDS View Entity, ZI_MM_PENALTIES, to read the domain values.

Tried by adding the below annotation

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity:{ name: 'ZI_MM_PENALTIES', element: 'Domvalue' } }]

in the extended CDS views of I_ServiceEntrySheetTP and C_ServiceEntrySheetWD 

However, I am unable to see the field as a drop-down.

Could you kindly guide me on how to make the extended field appear as a dropdown?

entry.title: Hi can you explain how you achieved currently we are facing same issue with the BDC PLEASE
Re: Fiori tiles Manage Banks for Cash Manager
entry.content: great and comprehensive question but alas no obvious or simple answers due to simplification, i presume.

entry.title: Re: SAP Master Data Integration with Field Service Manage...
entry.content:
Hi Simon,

You need to create service instance of MDI for each application. Lets take example

Master Data from Left to right

S4 Public Cloud >>Company Code>>SuccessFactors

You need two service instance

One for S4 Public Cloud

One for SuccessFactors

Now if there are other applications which are also interested in getting Company Code master data. Service Instance need to be created for each application.

I hope you got the point, if you found this helpful. Please accept the answer 

@Simon

entry.title: Why the CPO and the Office of the CPO Should Care About Finance
entry.content:
The role of the Chief Procurement Officer (CPO) has undergone a significant transformation in recent years. Traditionally viewed as an operational function focused on price negotiation and tactical sourcing, procurement is now recognized as a strategic pillar integral to the overall financial health and strategic direction of the organization.

The central argument of this document is that as the CPO's responsibilities expand to encompass value creation, risk management, and alignment with corporate financial goals, a robust understanding of finance becomes paramount. This necessitates a shift from a transactional mindset to a strategic partnership with the finance department.

In the competitive landscape of today’s business world, it is essential that CPOs grasp the critical importance of understanding finance. By integrating concepts like agency theory[1], resource dependence theory[2], and transaction cost economics[3], CPOs can align procurement goals with company finances, manage supplier relationships effectively, and streamline procurement processes. This integration delivers tangible benefits across key areas of the company and reinforces the core message that a collaborative relationship between procurement and finance is essential for achieving sustainable competitive advantage.

Enabling Cost Management and Efficiency

A core responsibility of the CPO is to drive cost management and efficiency improvements. To achieve this effectively, a deep understanding of financial principles is essential.

Understanding Financial Constraints

The CPO must possess a thorough understanding of the company's financial constraints, including budget cycles, capital allocation processes, and working capital management. This knowledge enables the CPO to align procurement strategies with available resources and financial targets.

For example, in the manufacturing industry, aligning procurement with budget cycles and implementing just-in-time inventory management can reduce inventory holding costs by up to 15%.

Contributing to Budget Accuracy

The CPO's office plays a vital role in providing accurate data for budgeting and forecasting. Precise forecasting of procurement costs, including direct materials, indirect expenses, and services, is critical for creating realistic and reliable budgets.

By leveraging historical data, market intelligence, and predictive analytics, the CPO can improve budget accuracy by up to 20% in industries such as healthcare, where precise cost control is paramount.

Justifying Procurement Investments

CPOs must be able to articulate the financial benefits of procurement investments using recognized financial metrics such as Return on Investment (ROI), Net Present Value (NPV), and payback period. A well-constructed business case, supported by rigorous financial analysis, is essential for securing funding for strategic initiatives such as procurement technology implementations or supplier development programs.

In the technology sector, demonstrating an ROI of 150% or higher for procurement technology investments is a common benchmark for success. This enables a well-documented method that stakeholders can consider to make a proper decision and can be backed by numbers.

Supporting Risk Mitigation

Risk mitigation is an increasingly important aspect of the CPO's role, and financial considerations are central to effective risk management.

Financial Risk Assessment of Suppliers

CPOs must conduct thorough financial risk assessments of key suppliers to ensure their financial stability and ability to consistently deliver goods and services. This includes analysing suppliers' financial statements, credit ratings, and market positions.

In the automotive industry, proactive assessment of supplier financial risk has been shown to reduce supply chain disruptions by as much as 30%.

Ensuring Compliance with Financial Regulations

Procurement activities must adhere to all relevant financial regulations and compliance standards to avoid fines, penalties, and legal liabilities. This includes ensuring compliance with anti-corruption laws, such as the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) in the United States, tax regulations like the General Anti-Avoidance Rule (GAAR) in Canada, and international accounting standards such as IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards).

In the pharmaceutical industry, rigorous compliance with financial regulations can significantly reduce legal and reputational risks, potentially by up to 25%.

Currency Risk Management

For organizations with global supply chains, currency fluctuations can have a significant impact on procurement costs. CPOs must work closely with finance to develop and implement strategies for managing currency risk, such as utilizing financial instruments or negotiating contracts in local currencies. Financial instruments are tools used in the financial markets for borrowing, lending, and investing.

Examples include options, futures, forwards, swaps, and other derivatives that can help hedge against fluctuations in currency exchange rates. Effective currency risk management can save organizations approximately 10% in costs, particularly in sectors like retail with extensive international sourcing.

Driving Value Creation

Beyond cost savings and risk mitigation, procurement can play a proactive role in driving value creation and innovation.

Demonstrating Procurement's Financial Impact

CPOs must effectively communicate the financial impact of procurement initiatives to key stakeholders, including senior management and the board of directors. This requires tracking and reporting key performance indicators (KPIs) such as cost savings, revenue growth, and return on assets.

In the energy sector, strategic sourcing initiatives and supplier relationship management programs have been shown to increase profitability by 5% or more. This not only highlights the strategic importance of procurement but also reinforces the core message that a strong procurement-finance partnership is vital for organizational success.

Collaborating on Innovation Funding

When procurement identifies opportunities for innovation, the CPO must collaborate closely with finance to secure funding and resources. This includes developing compelling business cases, presenting financial projections, and aligning innovation initiatives with the company's strategic priorities.

In the consumer goods industry, collaborative partnerships between procurement and finance have been shown to increase market share by as much as 12%.

Supporting Sustainable and Ethical Sourcing

Sustainable and ethical sourcing practices can enhance a company's brand reputation, increase customer loyalty, and drive sales growth. CPOs should work with finance to develop and implement sustainable sourcing strategies that align with the company's environmental, social, and governance (ESG) goals. For instance, sourcing materials from suppliers who adhere to environmentally friendly practices or fair labor standards can improve the company’s image.

In the fashion industry, sustainable sourcing has been shown to improve brand reputation by 40%, leading to a 10% increase in sales. This practice not only contributes to long-term value creation but also emphasizes the core message of aligning procurement with broader corporate financial and ethical goals.

Leveraging Financial Data for Decision Making

Data-driven decision-making is essential for effective procurement, and financial data is a critical input into this process.

Spend Analysis & Beyond

CPOs must leverage financial data to conduct comprehensive spend analysis, identify cost-saving opportunities, and reduce waste. This includes analysing spending patterns, identifying maverick spending, and negotiating better prices with suppliers.

In the telecommunications industry, effective spend analysis has been shown to cut procurement costs by 15%.

Integrating with FP&A

Procurement data should be seamlessly integrated with Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) processes to ensure alignment with the company's overall financial goals. This includes sharing data on procurement costs, supplier performance, and market trends.

In the aerospace sector, this integration has improved financial performance alignment by as much as 20%. This seamless integration highlights the importance of a strong procurement-finance partnership.

Technology and Digitalization

The use of technology in procurement and finance integration is crucial for enhancing efficiency, transparency, and data-driven decision-making. ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) systems streamline and centralize data from various departments, ensuring that procurement and finance are aligned in real-time. E-Procurement platforms automate the purchasing process, reducing manual errors and providing a clear audit trail for financial analysis. Data analytics tools, such as spend analysis software, help organizations identify spending patterns, monitor supplier performance, and uncover cost-saving opportunities. By leveraging these technologies, companies can optimize procurement processes, improve financial planning, and ultimately drive better business outcomes.

Conclusion

Integrating procurement and finance is essential for a competitive edge. The CFO should grasp the strategic value of procurement, while the CPO must appreciate finance's role. A strong bond with finance allows the CPO to enhance procurement's financial impact, reduce risks, generate value, and boost overall financial success. Regular cross-functional training, shared KPIs, and clear communication are recommended to promote collaboration and understanding. In summary, the close collaboration between procurement and finance departments is not just beneficial but imperative for achieving sustainable competitive advantage. CPOs and finance professionals are called to action to embrace this partnership and align their strategies for the greater financial health of their organizations.

 

[1] Relevant example: Describes potential conflicts when procurement prioritizes the lowest-priced suppliers without assessing their financial stability, potentially disrupting supply chains and impacting finance's budget accuracy.

[2] Relevant example: Dependence on a single supplier for critical components exposes the company to price volatility and supply disruptions, impacting finance's forecasting and working capital management.

[3] Relevant example: Automating invoice processing and reconciliation between procurement and finance reduces delays, errors, and administrative costs, lowering overall transaction costs

Hello Experts,

Danish Anwar

entry.title: Transferring Custom Pricing Field to Fiori Manage Prices (F4111)
entry.content:
Hi all!
I have created a custom field ZZLGORT of type LGORT (storage location) within the ZSD_KOMPAZ structure, which is appended to the KOMPAZ structure.

This allows the storage location data to be available in table A5xx as part of the pricing condition combination (ZZLGORT/MATNR) in transaction VK11.
Additionally, ZZLGORT has a custom search help.

I have also implemented Enhancement Implementations within MV45AFZZ and RV60AFZZ.

My Questions:

  1. How can I transfer the A5xx-ZZLGORT field, along with its values, to the Fiori app F4111 (Manage Prices)? Is this possible in any way?
  2. How can I ensure that this field also retains its search help within the Fiori application?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: graduation project : SAP Based Smart Job Tracking System Hello,
entry.content:
Hello,

The topic of my graduation project is to develop an SAP Based Smart Job Tracking System. In this project, I aim to optimise job tracking and management processes using the SAP ERP system. My goal is to create a system that allows users to plan and track their daily tasks and perform performance analysis via SAP GUI or a web/mobile interface.

Within the scope of the project, I plan to develop a solution that includes the following main functions:

SAP ABAP Based Job Tracking System: I plan to provide business logic and data management with SAP ABAP.
User Based Task Assignment and Tracking Module: It will be a module where tasks are assigned and tracked for users.
AI Supported Performance Analysis and Reporting: Performance predictions and analyses will be made using machine learning and data mining.
Web Interface: I will develop a user-friendly interface using SAP Fiori or an independent platform.
Database Integration (SAP HANA): I plan to use SAP's HANA system as a database.
Integration with Non-SAP Data with Web Services: Integration with non-SAP data sources using REST API.


I am currently in the idea phase of this project and I am not quite sure what steps I should take to get started. I have a lot of ideas for the project, but I honestly don't know how to realise them yet. I would like to find out what kind of resources I should turn to in order to gain in-depth knowledge about SAP, ABAP, SAP HANA, Web services and AI. I am especially looking for basic guides or step-by-step guides on these technologies. I would also like to learn about the possible challenges I might face in this project and get more suggestions.

I plan to use web services for external data integration in the project.

I would like to get your suggestions and feedback on the development of the project and its integration with SAP. I would be very grateful if you can provide guidance on which resources I should take a look at. Thank you very much in advance if you take your valuable time and reply.

Yours sincerely,
Zeynep

Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

Regards,
Mario

entry.title: 1 warehouse order for outbound with two activity areas
entry.content:
Looking for a way to pick from floor level and then high rack area. These are two activity areas so it creating two work orders 1 for each area. 
the problem is the operator is not done filling the pallet and it once he picks one work order hit asks him to drop it off in GI area. 
if it was 1 work order for 2 activity then he could continue picking without having to drop offf at GI area. 

is this possible to have 1 work order with many activity areas in 1 order

@Christian_Ries

entry.title: How to Migrate and Enable SAP CDC for SuccessFactors Candidate Authentication
entry.content:
With Q4 2024 release, SAP SuccessFactors has integrated with SAP Customer Data Cloud for external candidate registration and authentication. This integration introduces several notable features:

  • Social login with major Login providers
  • Phone number-based login
  • Two-factor authentication
  • Robust security capabilities with enhanced password policy management
  • Configurable login and registration pages.

In this blog, we’ll walk you through the migration process, key admin settings, and how to enable and customize SAP CDC-powered candidate account management. Learn how to seamlessly transition from native authentication to a more secure, flexible, and user-friendly login experience!

To facilitate this transition, SAP SuccessFactors has streamlined the migration process with automated workflows, minimizing manual effort for administrators. By selecting just a few checkboxes, admins can initiate the migration seamlessly. However, while the core migration is automated, some of the new functional add-ons require additional configuration, as they depend on customer-specific inputs and credentials

Steps to Enable SAP CDC powered Candidate account management

An admin option has been added to the "Manage Recruiting Settings" page with the title: Career Site with SAP Cloud for Customer Data Cloud for External Candidate.

Note: There is currently a limitation where this feature does not support one-time passwords or link-based email verification. This issue is being worked on, and updates on its availability will be provided in the release notes.

Step 1: Initiate Migration

To initiate the migration of configuration and candidate account data, the admin needs to select the first checkbox with title: Initiate Setup and Candidate Data Migration for SAP Customer Data Cloud. This migration involves, but is not limited to, the following items:

  • Consent Statements
  • Password Policy
  • Candidate Account Data
  • Email Templates for candidate welcome and password reset

Once this option is selected and saved, the user is presented with a confirmation dialog that details the data center and process completion notification information. Upon confirmation, the system acknowledges the migration request. To prevent disruptions to normal operations, the migration job is scheduled and executed during off-peak hours.

Step 2: Enable SAP Customer Data Cloud

After the migration is completed, an additional option is enabled and shown in the admin page: "Enable SAP Customer Data Cloud". Once this option is selected, the SuccessFactors tenant begins using SAP CDC screen sets for login, registration, and password reset functionality.

The completion of the above two steps seamlessly migrates the system from native authentication platform to SAP CDC integrated service.
We will now get into additional configuration steps in order to start customizing and enabling the new features.

Configuring Admin Roles and Admin pages:

To customize and add new SAP CDC-powered functionalities, a new RBP permission has been introduced for managing the admin page - “Manage Configuration in Recruiting for SAP Customer Data Cloud Authentication”

This permission must be granted to the appropriate role to allow customer admins to access the configurations.

Once the permission is assigned to an admin user, they will be able to access Manage Console Integration and External Candidate Authentication. This admin page includes three customization aspects, organized under three tabs.

  1. Console Integration:
    SuccessFactors Recruiting and SAP CDC uses SAML for candidate authentication. Both systems share metadata documents containing information like their endpoints and certificates, allowing them to verify each other's authenticity. This admin section involves reviewing and regenerating the metadata exchange for any issues. But the nice thing about this section is that as the SAML configuration is automated during the initial setup so admin doesn’t need to provide any initial configuration.

     

  2. Console User:

    The SAP Customer Data Cloud (CDC) Console is the access point to the SAP Customer Data Cloud. All configurations, settings, screen set customizations, password policies, and more can be managed through the CDC Console.

    This admin section allows customers to search for and add users as administrators to access the CDC Console. The added users receive an email where they can set up their credentials for the CDC Console. Admin access to the Gigya Console can be revoked by clicking the cross icon on the list page.

     

  3. Screen Mapping:

    SuccessFactors Career Site embeds SAP CDC widgets for candidate registration, authentication, and password reset functionalities. During the initial migration, the system configures default screens and links. On this admin page, the default mapping can be modified to point to new screens or screen sets. This is only necessary when a customer opts to leverage CDC's enhanced theming and other capabilities.

    Another notable setting on this page is "Allow candidates to create an account using their phone number." Enabling this allows candidates to create or sign in to their account using only their phone number, without requiring an email address. However, this requires the Short Messaging Service (SMS) feature to be enabled under the Manage Recruiting Settings page. Additionally, the SMS provider configuration must be completed in the CDC Console.

    With these steps, you can enable and customize SAP CDC-powered candidate account management. In the next blog, we will dive deeper into the configuration of other interesting functionalities, such as social login, phone number-based login, two-factor authentication, and enhanced password policies.

entry.title: "Auto-suggestion offer" when pushing space; QM02 - Action box
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

I'd like to ask you if you have any idea how to set automatic suggestion (to show history) of email address used in "Send email" functionality in "Action box" in QM02. 

I already tried to turn off and on history, delete history etc. (acc.to these instructions), unfortunately it still doesn't work.... 

Thank you very much in advance for any ideas. 

P.

entry.title: Re: Mobile First - Making the SAP IAS-based Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE) great again! 🎉
entry.content:
At some point the Oauth flow should redirect back to the mobile client via its URL Scheme or Deeplink.

Can you shed some light on that topic?

Also can you explain the below?

Assume i make a backend first, make a xsuaa instance on the same space. Bind the instance to the Backend app. Then make a the authorize call by using the clietnid&secret of the XSUAA. Make a simple website whole https link can be put in xsuaa config, this website will have the logic to redirect immediately to native Scheme or deeplink?

entry.title: SAP CHOTA TALKS 2025 (Q1) - Event Highlights
entry.content:
As the name suggests, SAP CHOTA TALKS was an amazing evening with over 75 participants. The knowledge acquired was too good to be true, a great combination of insights and deep discussions with  SAP community members.
Beginning with the lamp, lighting represents wisdom triumphing over ignorance and light chasing out darkness. This idea is encapsulated in the Sanskrit phrase "Tamaso ma jyotirgamaya".

Event started with overview on SAP Community . various SAP Events , upcoming events etc.. by @Ruthvik_Chowdary 

 Session 1 :  Modernization Options for SAP Analytics by Koundinya Karanam .

Session emphasizes on Key Modernization Pathways, Hybrid Analytics Architecture, Integration with Contemporary Technologies, and Implementation Considerations were the main topics of this seminar.

 Panel discussion on  "Jobs in the age of AI, distinguishing Hype from Reality" with  Dr. Mahesh Kumar CV  , Gupta Kandula  , Raghu Boddu,   & Immanuel Kolagani 

As part of the Panel below two questions covered.

Question 1: What specific skills will become more valuable as AI becomes more prevalent ?

Question 2 : How would you distinguish between jobs that AI will enhance versus those it might replace?

The first question focused more on AI-enhanced jobs and jobs at risk of replacement, particularly repetitive analysis and routine administrative tasks. Employment and Fundamental Technical Functions.
For question 2, the panel discussed their opinions and perspectives on technical literacy, human-centered skills, critical thinking and analysis, and creative and innovative thinking.

Session 2 : The Impact of AI on International Trade Presented by Dileep Kumar Saketi

Key Insights highlighted on Analysis of AI's role in reshaping global trade dynamics & emerging opportunities in international markets.

Session 3 : Integrated AI functionalities into BRIM by Swapna Borra covered on Comprehensive overview of AI integration into Business Revenue Intelligence Management (BRIM).

 

The event concluded on an exciting note with a fun lucky draw session for the attending participants. Using a spinning wheel to add an element of excitement and fairness, four fortunate attendees were selected to receive commemorative t-shirts as prizes. The lucky draw brought a festive atmosphere to the closing moments of the event, rewarding participants for their engagement and attendance.

Finally event is ended with the memorable group photo.

 

 

@Ruthvik_Chowdary @Vasavi_Rongali @KatherineKenneally @marco_v_santos @lauraschmitz

entry.title: Re: sybase asa 17 run webserver error webservice CALL PRO...
entry.content:
Did you test calling the procedure on its own rather than from an web service call? The TABLE REF requires a variable type TABLE REF and it cannot be a character string - it has to be a database object

i.e., TABLE REF( groupo.departments ) not TABLE_REF( 'groupo.departments').  This will need to be constructed with an indirect identifier not a TABLE REF as that is going to need to be declared as such before being passed to the procedure.

I assume that the HTTP request is just a typo for the variable name.  

create or replace procedure foo( in @table_name char(128) )
result( colval smallint ) )
begin
select 1 from `[@table_name]`;
end;

call foo('departments');

create or replace service foobar 
     type 'raw' authorization off 
     user dba 
     url on 
as 
     call foo(:table_name);

And the HTTP request

http://localhost:12345/foobar?table_name=departments

entry.title: SAP HCM S/4Hana Examination Inquiry
entry.content:
I am trying to access C_THR12_2311 examination for certificate. It's asking me to subscribe for the yearly plan of USD 1,332. 

Is there any cheaper alternative than subscribing for the yearly plan? Thank you.

P.S: I've found two exam chances for $250~ but not available for individuals.

entry.title: Re: How to get started as an SAP Builder
entry.content:
it's been a while since this post, 

is there a more up-to-date version of "getting started " ?

entry.title: Can MDF Object Be Linked to Goal and Development Form Templates?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently working on SAP SuccessFactors and want to understand if it’s possible to link an MDF object to Goal and Development form templates. Specifically, can MDF objects be referenced or integrated within these forms to store or display additional data? I

I’d appreciate any insights or best practices from those who have implemented similar use cases.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: In GB, there is no validation for item category when creating ad hoc requestion
entry.content:
In GB, there is no validation for item category when creating ad hoc requestion using toggle for Goods/Services. Users are able to raise service requisition using Goods category and likewise. Can someone please help why CEME validation does not work in GB and what are the possible ways to resolve this.

SAP Ariba Procurement

entry.title: Re: Exploring the Banner in the New Learning Page of SuccessFactors
entry.content: Email doesn't work when clicked after making live.  Any advice?

entry.title: Re: Como tonar campo(s) disponíveis para edição na transa...
entry.content:
Você pode verificar neste link abaixo onde exibe uma tela com a categoria de Imposto. Nesta tela existe a área control data a onde é possível definir que campo poderá ficar visível.

How to analyse incorrect or none withholding taxes accumulation | SAP Help Portal

Veja mais informações sobre Imposto retido na fonte aqui:
Passo a Passo da configuração e lançamentos dos Im... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Unable to Add a View Instead of a Fragment in Custom Section of Fiori App
entry.content: I have the same problem with a Fiore Elements Project. In the Objetct Page it is possible insert sections. But the section can only be fragments.

entry.title: Re: Issues with Recording Simulations in Microsoft Edge –...
entry.content:
Hi Funda,

Yes, I noticed the same issue late last week so I suspect that something has changed with the application.  Can you please raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and get support involved in the investigation.

Thanks
Shane

entry.title: Re: default values for action parameters from function in...
entry.content: Did you find a solution for this? Why can't SAP provide an easier way of down porting these new features to lower releases? 😐

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Situation type PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR not found
entry.content:
Hi @Mandy2025,

PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR is a predelivered situation template in the extended framework for Situation Handling. You'll find it available in the Manage Situation Types - Extended app. You can watch the video to learn more about the configuration process for this use case.

Kind regards,

Ana

entry.title: Re: Costs & Revenues getting mapped against separate WBS...
entry.content: To measure profitability by Work Package you need to create separate Billing Items for each work package.  This aligns the Work Package WBS to the Billing WBS 1:1.

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Situation type PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR not found
entry.content: @ana_goncalves My system not have "Manage Situation Types - Extended" app. Would u mind to enlighten me how to find this pls?

entry.title: Re: Onb Emails
entry.content:
Hello Danish,

This email template is coming from a HTML code which you already have access to in the email services

Navigation: Email services> Email settings > Email layout settings > Click on Check "Use Custom Layout"

Through this you will be able to see the html school. Using this html code you have to tweak this in a html editor. For just to play around you can copy the whole html code in to this testing site "https://www.w3schools.com/html/tryit.asp?filename=tryhtml_basic" just to see the preview of the code if you make changes in the code.

One thing you need to keep in mind. This layout change is for global which means whatever you change here will apply to all the existing email templates in your instance.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3426387/E

Hope this helps.

Regards,

Surya

entry.title: Re: Why can I find an employee/user in "hire date correct...
entry.content:
Hello @MortenSkovThomsen ,

Not sure if this is completely resolved but wanted to comment just in case the below info might be helpful.

Try doing the Soft Purge and Hard purge using the DRTM tool. Also I normally use this tool called "Data Inspector" where you can actually find the system info which includes the person ID of the purged user as well.

Data Inspector > Select Entity Table_USERS_SYSINFO > Add Filer Criteria with User sys ID/Username

This might be helpful to find the purged users as well.

Regards,

Surya

entry.title: Re: How to remove the delete option from Personal Data Co...
entry.content:
Hello @inepre1 ,

Did you tried restricting the RBP (employee central effective dated entities > Address > Remove the delete permissions)? 

You can just give Edit/Insert and Correct and remove the delete permissions for the external user permission role.

Hope this helps.

Regards,

Surya

entry.title: Re: In the BP tcode, I want to disable the Status tab.
entry.content:
My exact question? How much clearer can I be?

I repeat. I want to disable the BP Status tab for certain users using standard auth objects. Just like we do in MM02 using PSTAT values.

Not via enhancements, modifications, BADIs, user exits, screen exits, BTEs, core mods, customer functions, DME, etc., etc.

entry.title: Re: Maintain tolerance limits for tolerance key PE (CoCode S001)
entry.content: Thank you so much for solving the error It helped me a lot

entry.title: Re: People Storie positions only of bosses
entry.content:
Hello @Jhonatan ,

This might work as per your expectation. Try selecting the field values of the higher level position table  through the actual position table in the Query  designer. Please check the attached screenshot. You do have to select the code from main position object.

 

So after exporting the output in to a spreadsheet you will ignore/delete the position code values column of main position table and then leave the rest of the information.

Hope this helps.

Regards,

Surya

entry.title: Test Plan Execution Stuck at "Enter Application" – Coverage 0%
entry.content:
Hi,

I am encountering an issue while executing test plans in SAP. The test execution starts, but the Coverage remains at 0%. The test appears to be stuck at the "Enter Application" action.

Additional Details:

  • We have tried using different user accounts, but the issue persists.
  • The test execution was working fine last week.
  • I suspect an update over the past weekend/week might have affected execution.
  • I am still able to create new processes, but execution does not proceed.

Has anyone else experienced this issue, or is there a known fix/workaround?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Inquiry on Adding Customer Names in V_AISAPCUSTNOS
entry.content:
I am in the process of setting up a new Solution Manager to replace our current system, which will soon be decommissioned. As part of this migration, I have selectively transferred customer data from the legacy V_AISAPCUSTNOS table. However, while adding customer users, I am unable to retain the additional column information.

 

I came across SAP Note 1409328, "Customer Names in V_AISAPCUSTNOS," but I am unclear about the procedure for adding customer names. Could you please advise on how to resolve this issue?

 

Thank you for your assistance.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Inquiry on Adding Customer Names in V_AISAPCUSTNOS
entry.content:
Dear *,

 

Could you please check SAP Note to know about Customer Names in V_AISAPCUSTNOS?

##2182476 - Batch Job REFRESH_ADMIN_DATA_FROM_SUPPORT in Solution Manager 7.2 as of SP05

-------

If you have activated the multiple customer scenario or maintained SAP Customer Numbers in view V_AISAPCUSTNOS, the following data will be additionally downloaded to SAP Solution Manager:

  • SAP customer numbers
  • If you are the value-added resellers or customer centers of expertise, this report will download the customer numbers and their descriptions which have a relationship (VAR or CCC) with your customer number in SAP backbone. The data will be saved to view V_AISAPCUSTNOS.
  • If the generation of business partner is enabled, this job generates Business Partners of organizations for the corresponding customer numbers. The customer names are maintained into these Business Partners by this job.

-------

 

Best regards,

Leo

SAP Support
